description_long: This object is a classic Canon AT-1 film camera, featuring a robust metallic body with a textured black grip and silver top and bottom plates. The front displays a prominent Canon logo above the lens mount, and the attached lens is marked with detailed aperture and focus distance indicators. The top of the camera includes a film advance lever, shutter speed dial, and a hot shoe for flash attachment. The back of the camera is dominated by the viewfinder eyepiece and a textured grip surface, with a film door that likely opens for film loading. The lens barrel is large and protrudes from the body, with visible knurled rings for manual adjustment. The overall design is boxy and utilitarian, typical of vintage SLR cameras, with a mix of metal and plastic components.
description: A vintage Canon AT-1 film camera with a silver and black body, large manual lens, and classic SLR controls. The design is boxy, metallic, and features detailed dials and textured grips.
description_view_0: Front view showing the Canon logo, lens glass, and aperture markings on the lens barrel.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the lens focus and aperture rings, shutter button, and film advance lever.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the viewfinder eyepiece, film door, and textured grip surface.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the film rewind knob, shutter speed dial, and more of the lens barrel.
